blocks.content:
## Comparison Operators

Comparison operators create boolean values:

```javascript
5 > 3        // true   (greater than)
5 < 3        // false  (less than)
5 >= 5       // true   (greater than or equal)
5 <= 3       // false  (less than or equal)
5 == 5       // true   (equal to)
5 != 3       // true   (not equal to)
```

blocks.title: Comparison Operators
blocks.code:
let age = 16;

console.log(age > 18);      // false
console.log(age < 21);      // true
console.log(age >= 16);     // true
console.log(age == 16);     // true
console.log(age != 18);     // true

blocks.content:
## Logical Operators

```javascript
true && true    // AND: both must be true → true
true && false   // AND: both must be true → false
true || false   // OR: at least one must be true → true
false || false  // OR: at least one must be true → false
!true           // NOT: flips the value → false
!false          // NOT: flips the value → true
```

blocks.title: Logical Operators

blocks.instruction: Create a boolean variable that is false:
blocks.explanation: Boolean values are either true or false. No quotes needed!

questions.question: What is the result of 10 > 5?
questions.explanation: 10 is greater than 5, so the comparison 10 > 5 returns true.

questions.question: What is the result of true && false?
questions.explanation: The && (AND) operator requires both sides to be true. Since one side is false, the result is false.
